step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture's flows based on the user's interaction journey described in the transcript. Flow 0 represents user authentication and portal loading: Sanofi analysts access the frontend securely served by Amazon CloudFront from Amazon S3, using AWS Cognito federated with Sanofi SSO for single sign-on authentication. Flow 1 represents auditing: dynamic requests from the portal route through Amazon API Gateway to log activities and fetch user metadata in Amazon DynamoDB. Flow 2 represents data science workspace provisioning: requests from the portal trigger AWS Service Catalog to safely launch secure, isolated RStudio and Amazon SageMaker analytics environments, coordinated via AWS Lambda hooks. Flow 3 represents the NLP translation and de-identification pipeline: custom AWS Lambda functions leverage Amazon Translate and Amazon Comprehend Medical to parse medical data securely within the ecosystem.

graph.name: Sanofi Darwin - Real World Evidence (RWE) Data Analytics Platform
graph.notes: An enterprise analytical workspace platform built for Sanofi data scientists to securely analyze medical and Real World Evidence (RWE) data. It hosts static assets on S3 distributed via CloudFront, secures user authentication via AWS Cognito federated with Sanofi SSO, provisions reproducible analytical sandboxes (RStudio, SageMaker) via Service Catalog, and runs de-identification and translation pipelines using Lambda with Amazon Comprehend Medical and Amazon Translate.

nodes.name: Users
nodes.notes: Sanofi data scientists and analysts accessing the data analytics portal.

nodes.name: SANOFI SSO
nodes.notes: Sanofi corporate Single Sign-On Identity Provider federated with Cognito.

nodes.name: CLOUDFRONT
nodes.notes: CloudFront CDN serving the portal static website with SSL/TLS certificate offloading.

nodes.name: COGNITO
nodes.notes: AWS Cognito managing authentication and federation with Sanofi's identity provider.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: S3 bucket hosting the static website assets of the portal.

nodes.name: API GATEWAY
nodes.notes: Amazon API Gateway managing dynamic REST API endpoints for user and audit metadata.

nodes.name: DYNAMO DB
nodes.notes: Amazon DynamoDB storing user metadata and comprehensive platform audit logs.

nodes.name: SERVICE CATALOG
nodes.notes: AWS Service Catalog managing standardized analytical environment provisioning via CloudFormation.

nodes.name: LAMBDA
nodes.notes: AWS Lambda executing provisioning lifecycle scripts and analytical backend tasks.

nodes.name: R STUDIO
nodes.notes: Standardized RStudio analytical workspace provisioned per-user.

nodes.name: SAGEMAKER
nodes.notes: Amazon SageMaker notebook instances provisioned for individual ML development.

nodes.name: TRANSLATE
nodes.notes: Amazon Translate used for bulk document translation of clinical and medical text files.

nodes.name: COMPREHEND MEDICAL
nodes.notes: Amazon Comprehend Medical used to recognize medical entities and de-identify sensitive data.

nodes.name: DATA LAKE
nodes.notes: Secure landing zone and analytical data lake with strong access boundaries to prevent extraction.

edges.notes: Users access the web portal interface via CloudFront CDN.
CloudFront serves the static portal assets stored securely in S3.
Sanofi SSO federates user authentication with Amazon Cognito.
Cognito session token authorizes access to protected portal assets in S3.
Static portal frontend triggers dynamic API operations using API Gateway.
API Gateway records user actions and stores/reads metadata in DynamoDB for traceability.
User interacts with the frontend to trigger deployment of workspace sandboxes via Service Catalog.
Service Catalog provisions isolated RStudio server environments for statistics workloads.
Service Catalog spins up custom encrypted SageMaker Notebooks for users.
Service Catalog orchestrates lifecycle hooks using AWS Lambda functions.
Lambda triggers translation of bulk clinical documentation using Amazon Translate SDK.
Lambda invokes Comprehend Medical to detect PHI and de-identify raw files.
